other_observers: Loch Kilpatrick reported it on 14 Sep 2008
description: A small gull among Ring-billed and California gulls that flew past me undetected except that I unknowingly took one picture of it flying by.  It came into a pack of other gulls loafing and bathing in the SE corner of the reservoir, where I later found it swimming.  It was smaller than the nearby Ring-billeds, with a mantle about as dark as those of the Californias and sported dark amber eyes; a small, thin bill with a vague darker band near the gonydeal angle; large mirrors on both p9 and p10; and thick and well-defined scapular and tertial crescents.
